summary: Use keyboard-layout-resolved virtual-key events for the packaged Message walkthrough after a clean Sandbox proved ordinary physical-key input and disproved Unicode packet input.

decisions.decision: Keep the verified-process and focused-textarea boundaries from D-0453, but replace KEYEVENTF_UNICODE records with keyboard-layout-resolved virtual-key down/up records. Permit only mappings that require no modifier or Shift, fail closed on unsupported characters or Control/Alt mappings, and accept only bounded even sent-record counts before the independent React commit oracle runs.
decisions.rationale: The c1da348 exact guest helper sent every requested Unicode record but React retained no Message value. A separate clean Windows Sandbox session using the same c1da348 binary and fixed WebView2 150 runtime retained ordinary physical-key input and enabled Send. That isolates the remaining difference to Unicode packet versus physical virtual-key semantics; it does not reopen the exhausted WebDriver/CDP event permutations.

testEvidence.criterion: The helper must compile, map test text to ordinary virtual-key records, reject unsupported modifiers, expose no message content, and remain manifest-bound to the verified guest process.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused packaged-input wiring test, compile the real PowerShell/C# helper against a nonexistent PID, and perform one clean Windows Sandbox real-input check on the exact predecessor binary.

red.command: Exact clean packaged Windows Sandbox run bound to commit c1da348798fd378c185d2bc188ddaf9b9b969a81 and tree 620a0cdff747ca7916975b2f41da99d0fafe0c0b
red.outcome: RUNTIME RED: KEYEVENTF_UNICODE reported the full sent count but the packaged Message value remained empty and the mock received zero requests. Thirteen pre-message guest events and cleanup passed. No WebDriver/CDP permutation will be retried.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 focused test passed, 54 skipped. The helper source requires VkKeyScanEx/AddPhysicalCharacter, forbids KEYEVENTF_UNICODE, retains exact process binding, and validates bounded record-count evidence. The real helper C# compiled and rejected nonexistent PID 2147483646 before input.

testEvidence.criterion: A real clean Windows user must be able to enter a Message and reach the enabled Send state on the exact packaged binary.
testEvidence.test: Launch c1da348 scadmill.exe with its pinned fixed WebView2 150 runtime in networking-disabled Windows Sandbox, configure a synthetic local provider, focus Message, and send ordinary physical-key events without clicking Send.

red.command: Exact clean packaged Windows Sandbox run bound to commit c1da348798fd378c185d2bc188ddaf9b9b969a81 using KEYEVENTF_UNICODE
red.outcome: RUNTIME RED: the verified helper sent every Unicode record but the focused packaged Message field retained no text and Send stayed disabled.

green.command: Computer Use clean-Sandbox manual input walkthrough; evidence local://C:/Users/Scott/Documents/Codex/2026-07-09/read/outputs/scadmill-manual-input-c1da348-evidence-20260720.json
green.outcome: GREEN: the exact SHA-256 D61DD03AA4DCA79BF9275CAF2EB4F82B90F0259FB485C19E0DD8EBAD30E4A5A1 binary retained non-empty Message text and enabled Send. The message was not submitted.

nearMisses.sought: Exact clean packaged M4 walkthrough using Unicode SendInput records
nearMisses.confirmation: The helper activated the verified process and sent the full Unicode record count, but React retained no Message text and the mock received zero requests. The distinct physical-key hypothesis is allowed one source-bound clean run and will not be blindly rerun.

questions: No owner decision is required. One exact clean source-bound packaged run will decide this physical-key implementation.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
